role_assessment_summary.repair_instruction: Replace the repeated wireframe deck surface with a more inspectable finished product hero and fewer debug-colored markers.
Make the hero field visually distinct from product reveal pages; the route should drive a larger and more legible destination state.
Keep the seam but reduce debug-lens feeling; make before and after product states visibly different at thumbnail scale.
Turn the workspace into source, output, and review objects with readable labels, not repeated thin UI cards.
Differentiate climax from cover with scale, richer product content, and a decisive completion moment.
Keep decision nodes but enlarge labels and make the release decision visually dominant.

source_run2_75_renderer_repair.repair_gap_from_j: 2.75 increased product-surface detail but repeated the same wireframe product-window skeleton across core pages.

global_repair_strategy.from_failure_mode: wireframe blueprint aesthetic and repeated product surfaces
global_repair_strategy.target_shift: public-video presentation scene with differentiated page rhythm
global_repair_strategy.not_data_layer_reason: The required data, scene plan, visual grammar, renderer adapter, and text sockets are present; the visible failure is visual grammar differentiation and renderer expression.
page_differentiation_mandates: cover and climax must not both read as product surface diagrams
setup must not borrow proof workspace structure
proof must become an evidence inspection scene rather than a grid
close must resolve as a decision / release gate rather than a summary page
forbidden_renderer_fallbacks: same product window skeleton on every page
schematic blueprint as final visual unless it is the actual proof object
global_repair_strategy.quality_gate: K2 may start a renderer rerun only if every page has a distinct target scene direction and explicit removal rules for 2.75 wireframe artifacts.

source_j_assessment.repair_instruction: Replace the repeated wireframe deck surface with a more inspectable finished product hero and fewer debug-colored markers.

page_repair_plans.current_failure: The cover has more surface detail than 2.73 but still opens as a thin product surface diagram with debug-like red markers.
page_repair_plans.target_scene_direction: finished product hero emerging from source memory
page_repair_plans.visual_grammar_change: Keep product_reveal, but shift from diagrammatic product window to a cropped launch-deck hero object emerging from a softer memory field.
page_repair_plans.renderer_change: Use hero crop, editorial mask, asymmetric foreground/background depth, and a larger foreground product object with only edge-bound proof markers.
page_repair_plans.text_binding_adjustment: Keep the headline in a negative-space pocket and reduce proof labels to two or three meaningful edge attachments.
must_preserve_from_2_75: source trace terms absent from canvas
must_remove_from_2_75: same product window skeleton on every page

acceptance_checks.page_differentiation_check: Cover reads as an opening finished product hero, not a smaller version of the climax product reveal.
acceptance_checks.wireframe_reduction_check: No debug-colored outline language, repeated micro-panels, or thin schematic shell dominates the first read.
acceptance_checks.renderer_capability_check: Uses hero crop and editorial mask with foreground/background depth.

source_j_assessment.repair_instruction: Make the hero field visually distinct from product reveal pages; the route should drive a larger and more legible destination state.

page_repair_plans.current_failure: The setup route is present, but the destination object and thin labels make it feel close to product reveal and proof workspace pages.
page_repair_plans.target_scene_direction: source-to-memory transformation field with one visible route bend
page_repair_plans.visual_grammar_change: Strengthen hero_field as a transformation landscape: entry fragments, memory condenser, and destination proof object must have unequal scale and one clear route bend.
page_repair_plans.renderer_change: Use an editorial mask for the source side, asymmetric foreground/background depth for the destination side, and scene-specific connectors along the field route.
page_repair_plans.text_binding_adjustment: Replace scattered small annotations with one route label, one destination label, and one viewer-note overflow path.
must_remove_from_2_75: same product window skeleton on every page

acceptance_checks.page_differentiation_check: Setup reads as transformation before rendering, not as proof workspace or product reveal.
acceptance_checks.wireframe_reduction_check: Source fragments and route bend use masks and depth instead of thin debug outlines.
acceptance_checks.renderer_capability_check: Uses editorial mask and scene-specific connectors along the field route.

source_j_assessment.repair_instruction: Keep the seam but reduce debug-lens feeling; make before and after product states visibly different at thumbnail scale.

page_repair_plans.current_failure: The comparison seam is distinct, but the before/after states still feel like a schematic diagram study.
page_repair_plans.target_scene_direction: asymmetric before-after product theater with after-state dominance
page_repair_plans.visual_grammar_change: Keep before_after_theater, but make the after surface a foreground scene and reduce the seam to a supporting route-break lens.
page_repair_plans.renderer_change: Use asymmetric foreground/background depth, scene-specific connectors, and fewer but more meaningful labels attached to the after object.
page_repair_plans.text_binding_adjustment: Move delta copy to connector endpoints and route secondary detail to viewer notes instead of listing mechanism labels.
must_remove_from_2_75: schematic blueprint as final visual unless it is the actual proof object

acceptance_checks.page_differentiation_check: Contrast reads as a stage comparison, not a balanced two-card matrix.
acceptance_checks.wireframe_reduction_check: The seam is not the dominant debug object; before and after product states carry the argument.
acceptance_checks.renderer_capability_check: Uses asymmetric foreground/background depth and scene-specific connectors.

source_j_assessment.repair_instruction: Turn the workspace into source, output, and review objects with readable labels, not repeated thin UI cards.

page_repair_plans.current_failure: The proof page is more populated than 2.73 but still resembles a repeated thin-card QA grid.
page_repair_plans.target_scene_direction: evidence inspection scene with a larger active proof object
page_repair_plans.visual_grammar_change: Keep evidence_workspace, but make one angled inspection workbench and active lane the primary structure instead of a grid of equal proof cards.
page_repair_plans.renderer_change: Use a larger proof object, non-grid evidence arrangement, scene-specific connectors, and fewer but more meaningful labels pinned to the active lane.
page_repair_plans.text_binding_adjustment: Enlarge the decision/output labels and move secondary proof detail into viewer notes or metadata.
must_remove_from_2_75: same product window skeleton on every page

acceptance_checks.page_differentiation_check: Proof reads as an evidence inspection scene, not a grid, dashboard, or setup field.
acceptance_checks.wireframe_reduction_check: The active proof object is larger than secondary labels and avoids repeated thin card shells.
acceptance_checks.renderer_capability_check: Uses larger proof object and non-grid evidence arrangement.

source_j_assessment.repair_instruction: Differentiate climax from cover with scale, richer product content, and a decisive completion moment.

page_repair_plans.current_failure: The climax is denser than 2.73 but still too similar to the cover and lacks a finished reveal moment.
page_repair_plans.target_scene_direction: completion reveal of editable PPT result with full-frame payoff
page_repair_plans.visual_grammar_change: Keep product_reveal, but differentiate from cover by making the result object larger, more complete, and visually fused with source memory, code, and QA proof.
page_repair_plans.renderer_change: Use hero crop, asymmetric foreground/background depth, larger product surface, and fewer but more meaningful labels orbiting behind the result.
page_repair_plans.text_binding_adjustment: Keep only completion and editability labels on canvas; route proof detail to viewer notes or metadata.
must_remove_from_2_75: same product window skeleton on every page

acceptance_checks.page_differentiation_check: Climax reads as a completion reveal, not as a second cover product diagram.
acceptance_checks.wireframe_reduction_check: The editable result owns the frame and does not expose a schematic skeleton as the final visual.
acceptance_checks.renderer_capability_check: Uses hero crop and asymmetric foreground/background depth with a larger result object.

source_j_assessment.repair_instruction: Keep decision nodes but enlarge labels and make the release decision visually dominant.

page_repair_plans.current_failure: The close is distinct but still feels like a small-label summary map rather than a decision moment.
page_repair_plans.target_scene_direction: decision / release gate handoff map with one dominant blocked-public branch
page_repair_plans.visual_grammar_change: Keep decision_map, but make the release gate and next-run branch the primary scene instead of a distributed summary of labels.
page_repair_plans.renderer_change: Use scene-specific connectors, larger decision nodes, editorial mask around the blocked public branch, and fewer but more meaningful labels.
